info.title: Microsoft Excel REST API
info.description: Microsoft Graph API endpoints for working with Excel workbooks stored in OneDrive for Business, SharePoint sites, or Group drives. Provides CRUD operations on workbooks, worksheets, ranges, tables, charts, named items, and workbook functions.

tags.name: Sessions
tags.description: Manage workbook sessions for efficient API usage

tags.name: Ranges
tags.description: Read and write cell ranges within worksheets

tags.name: Tables
tags.description: Manage Excel tables, rows, and columns

tags.name: Named Items
tags.description: Manage named items (defined names) in a workbook

post.description: Create a new workbook session. Excel APIs can be called in persistent or non-persistent session mode. A persistent session saves changes to the workbook; a non-persistent session does not.

persistChanges.description: If true, changes are persisted (saved) to the workbook. If false, changes are not saved.

post.description: Close an existing workbook session.
Refresh an existing workbook session to keep it alive.

get.description: Retrieve a list of worksheets in the workbook.

post.description: Add a new worksheet to the workbook. The worksheet is added at the end of existing worksheets.

name.description: The name of the worksheet to be added.

get.description: Read the properties and relationships of a worksheet object. The worksheet can be identified by name or ID.

patch.description: Update the properties of a worksheet object.

delete.description: Delete the worksheet from the workbook.

get.description: Get the range object specified by the address.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: The address of the range in A1-style notation (e.g. A1:B2).

patch.description: Update the properties of a range object, including values, formulas, and number formats.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: The address of the range in A1-style notation (e.g. A1:B2).

get.description: Get the range object containing the single cell based on row and column numbers. The cell can be outside the bounds of its parent range, as long as it stays within the worksheet grid.

parameters.name: row
parameters.description: The zero-indexed row number of the cell.

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: The zero-indexed column number of the cell.

get.description: Get the smallest range that encompasses any cells that have a value or formatting assigned to them. If the worksheet is blank, returns the top left cell.

post.description: Clear range values, format, fill, or border.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: The address of the range in A1-style notation.

applyTo.description: Determines the type of clear action.

post.description: Insert a cell or a range of cells into the worksheet in place of this range, and shift the other cells to make space.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: The address of the range in A1-style notation.

shift.description: Specifies which way to shift the cells.

post.description: Delete the cells associated with the range.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: The address of the range in A1-style notation.

shift.description: Specifies which way to shift the cells.

post.description: Merge the range cells into one region in the worksheet.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: The address of the range in A1-style notation.

across.description: If true, merge cells in each row of the specified range as separate merged cells.

post.description: Unmerge the range cells into separate cells.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: The address of the range in A1-style notation.

post.description: Apply a sort operation on the used range of a worksheet.

get.description: Get a list of table objects in the specified worksheet.

post.summary: Create a table in a worksheet
post.description: Create a new table in the specified worksheet.

address.description: Range address for the data source (e.g. A1:D4).

hasHeaders.description: Whether the data has column headers.

get.description: Get a list of all table objects in the workbook.

post.summary: Create a table in the workbook
post.description: Create a new table. The range source address determines the worksheet under which the table is added.

address.description: Range address for the data source.

hasHeaders.description: Whether the data has column headers.

get.description: Read the properties and relationships of a table object.

patch.description: Update the properties of a table object.

get.description: Get a list of table row objects.

post.description: Add a new row to the table.

index.description: The zero-based index position of the new row. If null, the row is added at the end.

values.description: A 2D array of unformatted values of the table row.

delete.description: Delete a row from the table at the specified index.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The zero-based index of the row to delete.

get.description: Get a list of table column objects.

post.description: Add a new column to the table.

index.description: The zero-based index position of the new column.

values.description: A 2D array of unformatted values of the table column.

delete.description: Delete a column from the table.

parameters.name: column-id
parameters.description: The ID of the column.

post.description: Convert the table into a normal range of cells. All data is preserved.

get.description: Get the range object associated with the data body of the table.
Get the range object associated with the header row of the table.
Get the range object associated with the totals row of the table.
Get the range object associated with the entire table.

post.description: Clear all the filters currently applied on the table.
Reapply all the filters currently on the table.
Apply a sort operation on the table.
Apply a filter on the specified column.

parameters.name: column-id
parameters.description: The ID of the column.

post.description: Clear the filter on the specified column.

parameters.name: column-id
parameters.description: The ID of the column.

get.description: Get a list of chart objects in the worksheet.

post.description: Create a new chart in the worksheet.

type.description: The chart type (e.g. ColumnClustered, Line, Pie, etc.).

sourceData.description: The range address of the source data (e.g. A1:C4).

seriesBy.description: Specifies the way columns or rows are used as data series.

get.description: Read the properties and relationships of a chart object.

patch.description: Update the properties of a chart object.

get.description: Get a base64-encoded image of the chart scaled to fit the specified dimensions.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: The desired width of the resulting image.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: The desired height of the resulting image.

parameters.name: fittingMode
parameters.description: The method used to scale the chart to the specified dimensions.

post.description: Reset the source data for the chart.

sourceData.description: The range address of the source data.

seriesBy.description: Specifies the way columns or rows are used as data series.

post.description: Position the chart relative to cells on the worksheet.

startCell.description: The start cell, e.g. the top-left corner cell for the chart placement.

endCell.description: The end cell, e.g. the bottom-right corner cell.

get.description: Get the list of chart series.
Get a list of named item objects in the workbook.

post.description: Add a new named item to the workbook scope.

name.description: The name of the named item.

reference.description: The formula or range that the name refers to.

comment.description: The comment associated with this name.

get.description: Read the properties and relationships of a named item object.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the named item.

patch.description: Update the properties of a named item object.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the named item.

visible.description: Whether the named item is visible.

comment.description: The comment associated with this name.

get.description: Return the range object that is associated with the named item. Throws an exception if the named item type is not a range.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the named item.

get.description: Get the list of named items scoped to the worksheet.

post.description: Invoke an Excel workbook function. The function name is specified in the URL path. Function arguments are provided in the request body. Supported functions include standard Excel functions such as ABS, SUM, AVERAGE, VLOOKUP, PMT, etc.

parameters.name: function-name
parameters.description: The name of the Excel function to invoke (e.g. abs, sum, vlookup, pmt).

schema.description: The function parameters. Each function has its own set of required parameters (e.g. for PMT: rate, nper, pv).

oauth2.description: OAuth 2.0 authentication using Microsoft identity platform. Requires Files.Read or Files.ReadWrite permission scopes.

scopes.Files.Read.All: Read all files the user can access
scopes.Files.ReadWrite.All: Read and write all files the user can access

itemId.name: item-id
itemId.description: The unique identifier of the drive item (Excel file).

worksheetId.name: worksheet-id
worksheetId.description: The unique identifier or name of the worksheet.

tableId.name: table-id
tableId.description: The unique identifier or name of the table.

chartId.name: chart-id
chartId.description: The unique identifier of the chart.

sessionId.name: workbook-session-id
sessionId.description: The workbook session ID for efficient API calls. Obtained from the createSession endpoint.

id.description: The unique identifier for the worksheet. Read-only.

name.description: The display name of the worksheet.

position.description: The zero-based position of the worksheet within the workbook.

name.description: The display name of the worksheet.

position.description: The zero-based position of the worksheet within the workbook.

address.description: The range reference in A1-style, including the sheet reference. Read-only.

addressLocal.description: The range reference in the user's language. Read-only.

cellCount.description: Number of cells in the range. Read-only.

columnCount.description: Total number of columns in the range. Read-only.

columnHidden.description: Whether all columns of the current range are hidden.

columnIndex.description: Column number of the first cell in the range. Zero-indexed. Read-only.

formulas.description: Represents the formula in A1-style notation.

formulasLocal.description: Represents the formula in A1-style notation in the user's language and locale.

formulasR1C1.description: Represents the formula in R1C1-style notation.

hidden.description: Whether all cells of the current range are hidden. Read-only.

numberFormat.description: Represents Excel's number format code for the given cell.

rowCount.description: Total number of rows in the range. Read-only.

rowHidden.description: Whether all rows of the current range are hidden.

rowIndex.description: Row number of the first cell in the range. Zero-indexed. Read-only.

text.description: Text values of the specified range. Read-only.

values.description: Raw values of the specified range.

valueTypes.description: The type of data in each cell. Possible values: Unknown, Empty, String, Integer, Double, Boolean, Error. Read-only.

values.description: Raw values to set in the specified range.

formulasLocal.description: Formulas in A1-style notation in user's locale.

numberFormat.description: Number format codes for each cell.

columnHidden.description: Whether columns in the range are hidden.

rowHidden.description: Whether rows in the range are hidden.

id.description: The unique identifier for the table. Read-only.

showHeaders.description: Whether the header row is visible.

showTotals.description: Whether the total row is visible.

highlightFirstColumn.description: Whether the first column contains special formatting.

highlightLastColumn.description: Whether the last column contains special formatting.

showFilterButton.description: Whether filter buttons are visible at the top of each column header.

showHeaders.description: Whether the header row is visible.

showTotals.description: Whether the total row is visible.

index.description: The zero-based index of the row within the table's row collection.

values.description: Raw values of the specified row.

id.description: The unique identifier for the column. Read-only.

index.description: The zero-based index of the column within the table's column collection.

name.description: The name of the table column.

values.description: Raw values of the specified column.

id.description: The unique identifier for the chart. Read-only.

height.description: The height, in points, of the chart.

width.description: The width, in points, of the chart.

left.description: The distance, in points, from the left side to the worksheet origin.

top.description: The distance, in points, from the top edge to the top of row 1.

height.description: The height, in points, of the chart.

width.description: The width, in points, of the chart.

left.description: The distance from the left side.

top.description: The distance from the top edge.

name.description: The name of the object. Read-only.

comment.description: The comment associated with this name.

scope.description: Indicates whether the name is scoped to the workbook or a specific worksheet. Read-only.

type.description: The type of reference associated with the name.

value.description: The formula that the name is defined to refer to. Read-only.

error.description: Error information if the function call failed.

value.description: The result value of the function call.

key.description: The column or row index to sort on (zero-based).

ascending.description: Whether to sort in ascending order.

color.description: The color used for sorting if sortOn is CellColor or FontColor.

icon.description: The icon used for sorting if sortOn is Icon.

matchCase.description: Whether case matters for string sorting.

orientation.description: Whether to sort top-to-bottom or left-to-right.

method.description: The ordering method used for Chinese characters.

operator.description: The operator for combining criterion1 and criterion2.

values.description: The set of values to filter.
